danger_signals.visual_form: Corporate lab environments, industrial aesthetic, mysterious genetic experiment chambers
danger_signals.meaning: Central threat and oppressive world-state; DM controls resources and conducts unethical experiments

danger_signals.visual_form: Aggressive enemy types throughout levels, distinct from civilian NPCs
danger_signals.meaning: Active danger from corporate enforcement; immediate combat threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Stalag 17 level (ruined DM labs), decayed urban settings
danger_signals.meaning: World-state of conflict aftermath; evidence of 2011 'economic wars'

danger_signals.visual_form: Narrative references to imprisoned sister Maria, kidnapped sister Amy, imprisoned Echo
danger_signals.meaning: DM's predatory control mechanisms; motivation for player action

safety_signals.visual_form: Four initial playable characters (Flynn, Echo, Al, Prokop) with distinct silhouettes and visual identity
safety_signals.meaning: Safe to trust these characters; they share unified opposition to DM

safety_signals.visual_form: Orbot/Metal Frame—yellow-painted combat robot with distinct mechanical design
safety_signals.meaning: Trustworthy despite corporate origin; actively opposes DM from within

safety_signals.visual_form: Referenced character who rescued and adopted Echo; surrogate father to Flynn
safety_signals.meaning: Moral anchor and sympathetic force within narrative (though deceased in backstory)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Corporate branding in lab environments, genetic experiment facilities
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: DM thugs, DM enforcers, multiple Orbots (hostile versions of player's robot ally)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stalag 17 ruined labs, industrial facilities, mysterious genetic experiment chambers

faction_indicators.color_identity: Yellow (Orbot's distinctive paint scheme signals defection and player alliance)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Personal vendetta items: Prokop's red armband (sister Amy's keepsake), character backstories worn as identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban streets, combat zones where resistance operates; branching paths indicate multiple strategic approaches

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Fnord enemies (weakest, most prolific early-stage type); Lord J reference (Brawl Brothers cameo, Musashi Plaza)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct enemy archetypes with own strengths/weaknesses; mini-boss Orbots distinct from player's Orbot
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Musashi Plaza (suggests urban gang/street presence); varied enemy types tied to location

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ruined environments, industrial decay, corporate monopoly control of world resources
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: DM conducts unethical genetic experiments; world population subject to corporate oppression and forced servitude

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Laboratory and facility-based level design; corporate infrastructure prominent; genetic experiment chambers visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: DM kidnaps individuals (Maria, Amy), imprisons dissidents (Echo), experiments on unwilling subjects (Flynn, Al)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Urban street levels, combat zones, branching level paths indicating strategic choices
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Four protagonists actively hunt DM; multiple character routes available; branching paths with different bosses and cut scenes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Orbot introduced as yellow-painted combat unit; distinct from other hostile Orbot mini-bosses
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Scientist defects and smuggles parts to build anti-DM Orbot; Orbot joins player faction; hostile Orbots become mini-boss encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character visual identity tied to loss markers (Prokop's red armband; Echo's youthful but combat-ready appearance)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Each character driven by specific DM atrocity (mentor murdered, sibling kidnapped, brother disappeared, sister enslaved)

environmental_storytelling.example: Stalag 17 (ruined DM labs) communicates corporate scale and destruction; Musashi Plaza hosts Lord J cameo, suggesting street-level resistance
Fnord enemies (weakest, most prolific) suggest low-level DM foot soldiers; mini-boss Orbots indicate DM's military manufacturing capacity
Multiple endings and branching gameplay paths suggest different strategic outcomes against DM; each path has unique bosses and cut scenes
Echo's prison rescue, Flynn's mentor murder, Al's brother disappearance, Prokop's sister kidnapping—all tied to DM actions; collectively illustrate systematic oppression
Orbot's yellow paint and smuggled construction materials visually communicate internal DM resistance; becomes trusted ally through visible defection

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Transforms into muscle-bound super being; visual manifestation of experimental enhancement by DM
Quick movements but weak attack power; behavioral compensation for trauma (imprisoned youth); executes Frankensteiner throws
Primarily uses grabbing moves and piledriver attacks; carries bazooka on back; strategic rather than aggressive combat
Slow but extremely powerful; wears red armband as constant visual reminder; channels electricity in wrestling throws
Lost shin bandages from previous game; German suplex now hits twice; multiple punch combo ending in Dragon Punch—escalating aggression
Easier enemy-grabbing due to long arms; uses blue flame burns; shoots bullets; distinguished from hostile Orbot mini-bosses by yellow paint and alliance
Appear throughout game as mini-boss encounters; mechanically mirror player's Orbot but represent corporate control; visual and behavioral opposition
Each with own strengths and weaknesses; responsive to location and level progression; abundant in early stages as Fnord enemy type

notes: The Peace Keepers uses a cyberpunk-adjacent world-state (post-economic war 2015, corporate monopoly) communicated primarily through narrative exposition and environment design rather than explicit visual symbolism. Danger manifests as DM corporate presence and infrastructure. Safety signals emerge from character alliances and shared vendetta. Faction identity relies on narrative role and character backstory rather than color-coding or sigils. The game's branching paths and multiple endings create a variable world-state manifestation based on player choices. Defector symbolism (Orbot, scientist) is key to communicating internal resistance. Character behavioral tells closely tie to trauma and motivation, with physical transformations (Flynn's super form) visualizing DM's experimental effects.
